Subject: Welcome to the Oakville Maker Collab!

Dear Ms. Lin,

Welcome to the Oakville Maker Collab! We are thrilled that you have joined our community of local hobbyists, DIY enthusiasts, and creators. Whether you are an experienced woodworker or just starting to learn about 3D printing, our facility is equipped to help you bring your personal projects to life.

As an active member, you now have full entry to our main workshop during regular operating hours (Tuesday through Sunday, 10:00 AM to 8:00 PM). Your membership also includes a weekly email digest featuring upcoming project showcases, safety reminders, and a schedule of our beginner-friendly weekend tutorials. Additionally, you can use our digital reservation system to book specialized equipment, such as the laser cutter or the heavy-duty sewing machines, up to two weeks in advance.

Before your first visit, we require all new members to complete a brief online safety module. Please log in to your member portal using the temporary credentials provided in your receipt, watch the 15-minute safety video, and sign the digital waiver. Once this is done, your electronic key fob will be automatically activated, allowing you to scan into the building.

We look forward to seeing what you create! If you need technical support with your online account, please reply directly to this email.

Warmly,
The Oakville Maker Collab Team

Notice: Spring Green Campus Swap Market

Date: Friday, May 10, 2024
Time: 10:00 AM – 3:00 PM
Location: Student Union Quad (Rain location: University Gymnasium)

Hosted by the Oakridge University Office of Sustainability, the Spring Green Campus Swap Market is a fun, eco-friendly way to refresh your wardrobe, find dorm essentials, and reduce campus waste before the semester ends!

How it works:
• Drop off your gently used clothing, textbooks, small electronics, and room decor at the Sustainability Center by Wednesday, May 8.
• For every item you donate, you will receive one "Swap Token."
• Bring your Swap Tokens to the market on Friday to exchange them for new-to-you items.
• Don't have anything to donate? No problem! Students can also purchase items on the day of the event for $2 each, with all proceeds going toward the campus community garden.

Item Guidelines:
Please ensure all clothing is washed and free of stains or holes. Electronics must be in working condition. We cannot accept large furniture items, undergarments, or opened food products.

Volunteer Opportunities:
We need student volunteers to help sort items, manage the checkout tables, and assist with cleanup. Volunteers who work a minimum two-hour shift will receive early access to the market at 9:00 AM and three bonus Swap Tokens. Sign up at www.oakridge.edu/sustainability/volunteer.

Department of Biological Sciences
Fall Semester Laboratory Access Protocol

Attention all students enrolled in 200-level and 300-level biology laboratory courses: Please review the updated safety and access guidelines for the upcoming Fall semester.

Mandatory Safety Training:
All lab participants must complete the online Chemical Hygiene Module via the university portal. The deadline for completion is September 12. Building access keycards will be activated for the science wing only after this module is successfully completed.

Equipment and Attire:
Please note a change in policy: Lab coats and safety goggles are not provided by the department this semester. Students are required to purchase their own approved protective gear at the University Bookstore prior to their first lab session. Closed-toe shoes and long pants remain strictly required at all times.

Facility Rules:
Access hours for the laboratory wing are 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM, Monday through Friday. Weekend access requires special faculty authorization. Furthermore, food and beverages are strictly prohibited inside all laboratories. Students must store their backpacks, snacks, and water bottles in the lockers located in the main hallway. Please bring your own combination lock to secure your belongings.

PixelPainter @pixelpainter • 4h

I finally finished my first digital portrait using the new sketching tablet I got last month! Transitioning from traditional watercolor to digital art has been way more challenging than I expected. Figuring out all the different layer settings and custom brushes took weeks of trial and error.

It's definitely a bit rough around the edges, especially the lighting on the hair, but I'm just glad I pushed through the frustration instead of giving up.

To all the digital artists out there: what was the hardest part of the learning curve for you? Any recommendations for good beginner brush packs? Let me know below!

#digitalart #learningcurve #illustration

Riverside College Spring Short Film Festival

Theme: "Unseen Perspectives"
Submission Deadline: March 10, 2025

Are you an aspiring filmmaker or a storyteller looking to share your vision? Submit your original project to the Riverside College Spring Short Film Festival!

Guidelines:
• Participation is restricted to currently enrolled undergraduate and graduate students at Riverside College.
• Films must have a total runtime of exactly five minutes or less, including credits.
• At least 50% of the footage must be filmed on the Riverside College campus.
• All audio, including music and sound effects, must be original or royalty-free. Copyrighted tracks will result in automatic disqualification.

Awards:
• Grand Prize: $1,000 grant towards film equipment and a guaranteed screening at the downtown Metro Theater.
• Audience Choice: $500 campus bookstore credit.

Submission Process:
Upload your video file (MP4 format only) via the student portal at www.riverside.edu/filmfest. Along with your video, please upload a brief director's statement (maximum 200 words) explaining the inspiration behind your work.

Finalists will be notified via email on April 2, 2025.

Elmwood College Bike-Share Program Update

Effective March 1st, the Elmwood College Bike-Share Program is getting a major upgrade! To help students more easily navigate the hilly terrain of the North Campus, we are replacing our entire fleet of standard bicycles with new electric-assist bicycles (e-bikes).

Along with the new bikes, please note the following changes to our pricing and parking policies:

• Pricing: The first 30 minutes of each ride will remain free (previously 60 minutes). After the initial 30 minutes, riders will be charged $0.15 per minute.
• Docking: All e-bikes must be returned and locked at designated "Charging Hubs," which are clearly marked with green paint on the pavement. Leaving an e-bike at a standard campus bike rack prevents it from charging and will result in an automatic $5 penalty fee applied to your student account.
• Safety: While riding e-bikes, helmets are strongly recommended. Students may pick up a free safety helmet at the Student Union help desk upon presenting a valid student ID.

For a map of all new Charging Hub locations, please visit the Elmwood College Transportation Services website.

From: Alex Rivera (alex.rivera@oakridge.edu)
To: Cycling Club Members (cycling-club@oakridge.edu)
Subject: Upcoming Sunday Ride to Harbor Point

Hi Everyone,

I'm excited to announce our next group ride scheduled for this Sunday, October 12th. We will be heading to Harbor Point, which offers some of the best ocean views in the region.

The route is approximately 25 miles round-trip and is considered moderate in difficulty due to a few steep hills near the halfway point. We will meet in front of the Student Union at 8:30 AM to check gear and will depart promptly at 9:00 AM.

Please remember that helmets are strictly required for this ride; for safety reasons, anyone without a helmet will not be permitted to join the group. We also strongly recommend bringing a spare inner tube and plenty of water, though these are not mandatory conditions for participation.

We plan to stop for a one-hour lunch break at the Harbor Point park. You are welcome to pack your own lunch, or you can purchase food from the various food trucks that are usually parked nearby on weekends.

If the weather forecast predicts heavy rain on Sunday morning, please check your email by 7:00 AM for a cancellation notice.

Please RSVP by Friday afternoon so we know how many riders to expect.

See you on the road,
Alex Rivera
President, University Cycling Club

Subject: Join Us for the First Annual Midtown Repair Café!

Dear Residents,

Before you throw away that broken toaster or torn jacket, bring it to the Midtown Repair Café! On Saturday, October 22nd, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM, we are hosting a free community event at the Oak Street Community Center.

Our goal is to reduce waste and teach valuable maintenance skills. Volunteer fixers will be stationed at different tables to help you repair small household appliances, bicycles, clothing, and toys. We provide the tools and basic materials, like thread, glue, and wire. However, if your item requires a specific replacement part—such as a specialized bicycle tire tube or a unique vacuum filter—please purchase it beforehand and bring it with you.

Wait times may vary depending on attendance, so we will offer complimentary coffee and pastries in the waiting area. If you are handy with electronics or sewing and would like to volunteer as a fixer, please reply to this email by Wednesday.

We look forward to fixing things together!

Sincerely,
The Midtown Neighborhood Association

From: Campus Recreation Coordination (recreation@oakridge.edu)
To: All Registered Students
Subject: Upcoming Event: Urban Photography Walk

Dear Students,

Are you interested in improving your camera skills while exploring our city's historic downtown? Join us for the upcoming Urban Photography Walk on Saturday, October 14th. This event is designed for amateurs and hobbyists who want to learn how to capture better architectural and street photos.

The walk will begin at 10:00 AM at the Main Street Plaza fountain and will conclude around 1:00 PM at the Riverside Cafe. Our guide will be Marcus Thorne, a professional photojournalist who frequently contributes to the City Tribune. He will share practical tips on framing, lighting, and capturing motion.

Participants must bring their own cameras. While smartphones with high-quality lenses are acceptable, a DSLR or mirrorless camera is highly recommended to get the most out of Marcus's technical advice. Please ensure your batteries are fully charged and you have enough storage space on your memory cards. Wear comfortable walking shoes, as we will be covering approximately three miles.

The registration fee is $15, which covers the cost of the guide and a complimentary coffee or tea at the end of the route. Space is limited to 15 students, so please sign up through the Campus Rec portal by Wednesday, October 11th.

Best regards,
Campus Recreation Team

Let me know if you want to walk over there together!

Best,
Mark

From: Maya Lin, Resident Advisor
To: 3rd Floor Residents, Oak Hall
Subject: Important: Winter Break Checkout Procedures

Hello everyone,

As winter break approaches, please remember that Oak Hall will officially close for the semester on Friday, December 15th at 5:00 p.m. All residents must vacate their rooms by this time.

Before you leave, you must complete a few checkout tasks. Please empty and clean your refrigerator, unplug all electronics (except the refrigerator), take out your trash, and ensure all windows are fully closed and locked.

If you are leaving before the 15th, please place your room key in an express checkout envelope and drop it in the designated box at the front desk. If you are leaving on Friday, I will be conducting final room checks between 3:00 p.m. and 5:00 p.m., and you can hand your key directly to me.

If your flight schedule requires you to stay past the 5:00 p.m. deadline, you must email the housing office by this Wednesday to request a late departure extension. Have a wonderful winter break!

Spring Semester Guide

Welcome to GreenWheels, the university's sustainable transit solution. Our program offers affordable, eco-friendly transportation for navigating the campus.

Rental Rates:
• Daily: $5
• Weekly: $20
• Full Semester: $75

What's Included:
Every rental comes with a safety helmet, a heavy-duty U-lock, and complimentary basic maintenance (such as chain oiling and tire inflation) at the Student Union Hub.

Important Policies:

• A $15 replacement fee applies for any lost U-lock keys.
• Bicycles must be secured only to designated green racks located across campus.

Note: GreenWheels rentals are exclusively available to actively enrolled students and current faculty members. A valid university ID must be presented at the time of pickup.

Subject: Welcome to the Midtown Makerspace Community!

We look forward to seeing what you create. If you encounter any issues logging in, please reach out to our support team.

Best regards,
Midtown Makerspace Administration

David

Course: Introduction to Creative Writing (ENG 250)
Instructor: Professor Julian Vance
Semester: Spring 2025

Course Description:
This introductory workshop focuses on the fundamentals of short fiction and poetry. Students will read published works, write original pieces, and critique their peers' writing in a collaborative and supportive environment.

Grading Breakdown:
• Portfolio (Final Project) – 40%
• Peer Critiques – 30%
• Weekly Reading Responses – 20%
• Attendance – 10%

Important Policies:
• Workshop Etiquette: Constructive feedback is required during peer review sessions. Disrespectful or overly harsh comments will result in immediate dismissal from the session and a zero for that day's participation.
• Late Submissions: Because the class relies on live discussion, peer critiques cannot be accepted late under any circumstances. Other assignments, such as reading responses, will incur a 5% grade penalty for each day they are late.
• Revision Policy: Students are allowed to revise one short story for a higher grade, provided the revision is submitted before the final week of classes.

Office Hours: Mondays and Wednesdays, 10:00 AM – 11:30 AM, Humanities Building, Room 405.

University Recreation Center: Fall Semester Group Fitness

From: University Housing Facilities (facilities@maplewood.edu)
To: All Residents of Maplewood Hall
Subject: Scheduled Water Shutoff and Routine Maintenance

Dear Residents,

Please be advised that there will be a scheduled water shutoff in Maplewood Hall on Tuesday, October 24th, from 8:00 AM to 2:00 PM. During this time, our maintenance crew will be upgrading the building's main plumbing line to prevent pipe freezing during the upcoming winter months.

While the maintenance is underway, there will be no running water in the building. This means that all apartment sinks, showers, and toilets will be inoperable. Additionally, the ground-floor laundry room will be temporarily closed.

To minimize inconvenience, complimentary bottled drinking water will be available at the front reception desk. Furthermore, Maplewood residents have been granted temporary access to the facilities in the adjacent building, Oakwood Hall. You may use your student ID card to enter Oakwood Hall and access their restrooms and showers on the first floor.

Important: Please ensure that all faucets in your apartment are completely turned off before you leave for classes on Tuesday morning. This will prevent accidental flooding when the water supply is suddenly restored in the afternoon.

Thank you for your cooperation and understanding.

Sincerely,
Facilities Management Team

Elena Rostova

Announcing the Green Commute Initiative at Northbridge University

The Office of Sustainability is launching a new program to reduce campus traffic and promote eco-friendly travel options. Starting September 15, the Green Commute Initiative will reward students and staff who choose alternative transportation methods, such as carpooling, public transit, cycling, or walking.

How to Participate:
• Registration: Create an account on the Northbridge Commuter portal. Log your daily trips to earn points.
• Rewards: Accumulate 50 points to receive a $10 voucher for the campus dining hall. Reach 200 points to earn a premium parking pass for days you must drive.
• Carpool Matching: Looking for a ride? Use the portal's secure matching tool to find peers traveling from your neighborhood.
• Transit Subsidy: Students logging at least 10 bus trips per month qualify for a 25% discount on their next monthly city transit pass.

Need help getting started? Visit the Sustainability Kiosk in the Student Union building on Tuesdays and Thursdays between 11:00 AM and 2:00 PM for assistance setting up your profile, or to pick up a free reflective safety band for walking and cycling during evening hours.

Participants need to bring their own sketchbooks, but the $25 student registration fee covers a travel-sized watercolor set and two water brushes that we get to keep. Let me know if you'd like to join! The deadline to sign up is Friday at noon. If you decide to come, we can just walk over to the downtown area together from the campus library.

Best,
Mark

Notice: Updates to Student Printing Quotas and Kiosks

Sender: Oakridge College IT Helpdesk
Date: December 5, 2024

Starting next semester (Spring 2025), Oakridge College will transition to a new campus-wide printing system designed to reduce paper waste and improve machine efficiency across campus.

New Quota System:
Instead of the previous unlimited black-and-white printing policy, each enrolled student will receive a $25.00 printing credit per semester. Black-and-white pages will now be deducted at a rate of $0.05 per page. Color pages, which previously required a separate prepay card, will cost $0.25 per page and can be deducted directly from your main printing balance.

Unused credits will roll over from the Fall to the Spring semester, but please note that all remaining balances will expire at the end of the academic year in May.

Adding Funds:
If you exhaust your $25.00 semester allowance, you can add additional funds to your account via the online student portal. Alternatively, you can add funds in person at the library circulation desk using a debit or credit card. Please be aware that cash is no longer accepted at any printing kiosk or service desk.

Equipment Upgrades:
Three new high-speed color printers have been installed in the Student Union lounge, replacing the outdated, frequently malfunctioning machines. The printers located on the second floor of the library remain unchanged but have been connected to the new quota network.

For technical issues or to dispute a charge for a misprinted page, please contact the IT Helpdesk or submit a support ticket online.

Notice of Phased Campus Wi-Fi Upgrade: Residence Halls

To All Residential Students,

Beginning October 3rd, University Information Technology Services (UITS) will be conducting a phased upgrade of the wireless network across all campus residence halls. This upgrade will transition our infrastructure to the new 'EduNet-Fast' protocol, providing significantly faster download speeds and improved connection stability during peak evening hours.

Schedule of Outages:
To minimize academic disruption, the physical installation of new routers will occur on a rolling, building-by-building basis between 9:00 AM and 2:00 PM. During these specific hours, students in the affected buildings will experience a total loss of Wi-Fi connectivity.

• North Quad (Lincoln, Hayes, and Grant Halls): Tuesday, October 3
• South Quad (Washington and Jefferson Halls): Wednesday, October 4
• West Campus (Franklin Tower and The Commons): Thursday, October 5

Action Required:
Once your building's upgrade is complete, you will need to manually forget the old 'CampusNet' network on your laptops and smartphones, and then log in to the newly available 'EduNet-Fast' using your standard student email and web portal password.

Please note that smart TVs and gaming consoles cannot authenticate this way; they will require a one-time MAC address registration via the UITS portal before they can successfully connect to the new network.

If you still experience connectivity issues after 3:00 PM on your designated upgrade day, please submit an online help ticket at uits.university.edu/helpdesk.

After five weeks of gluing my fingers together, my first stop-motion animation is finally rendering! It's a two-minute short about a robot exploring a junkyard, and the best part is that I built all the sets using 100% recycled materials from around my dorm—mostly cardboard boxes, soda cans, and bottle caps.

Since I didn't have professional equipment, I just used my smartphone and a couple of desk lamps. Lighting was definitely the most frustrating part of the process because the shadows kept shifting whenever I bumped the table.

I'm planning to start a new project next month. If anyone has cheap DIY lighting hacks or smartphone camera tips, please drop them in the comments!

Campus Sustainability: Community Garden Plot Lottery

The Office of Sustainability is pleased to announce that applications for the Spring Semester Community Garden plots are now open. Located behind the Life Sciences building, the garden offers 40 raised beds for students, faculty, and staff to grow their own vegetables and flowers.

Because interest in the garden has grown significantly, plots will be allocated via a lottery system rather than on a first-come, first-served basis. Interest forms must be submitted online by February 10. Applicants selected in the lottery will be notified by email on February 15 and must confirm their spot within 48 hours.

Guidelines for Gardeners:
• Organic Methods Only: The use of synthetic pesticides or herbicides is strictly prohibited to protect local pollinators.
• Maintenance: Gardeners must weed and water their assigned plots weekly. Neglected plots may be reassigned to those on the waiting list.
• Community Work Days: All participants are required to attend at least one Saturday cleanup event during the semester to help maintain shared pathways and tool sheds.

A non-refundable fee of $15 is required upon confirmation to cover the cost of water usage and shared equipment.

questions.stem: In the second paragraph, the word "allocated" is closest in meaning to

options.A: Constructed
options.B: Distributed
options.C: Expanded
options.D: Fertilized

questions.explanation: 'Allocated' means distributed or assigned to individuals.

questions.stem: Why has the university decided to use a lottery system for the garden plots?

options.A: To ensure that only Life Sciences students can participate
options.B: Because the number of interested people exceeds the number of available plots
options.C: To select the most experienced gardeners for the raised beds
options.D: Because the application deadline was extended by the administration

questions.explanation: The lottery was introduced because 'interest in the garden has grown significantly,' implying demand exceeds supply.

questions.stem: What is the likely consequence if a participant fails to care for their garden bed?

options.A: They will be required to pay an additional fine.
options.B: They will be banned from the Life Sciences building.
options.C: The university will hire staff to water it for them.
options.D: They may lose their right to use the plot.

questions.explanation: 'Neglected plots may be reassigned to those on the waiting list.'

items.text:
University Bulletin Board: Student Announcements

From: Campus Green Team <greenteam@oakridge.edu>
Subject: Help Us Wake Up the Garden!

Hi everyone,

It's finally spring! The Campus Community Garden has been dormant all winter, and it's time to get it ready for the growing season. We're hosting a 'Garden Kickoff' this Saturday, March 18th, from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM.

We'll be weeding, turning the soil, and fixing the wooden planters. Don't worry if you've never gardened before—our senior members will demonstrate everything you need to know. Just bring a water bottle and wear closed-toe shoes for safety. We've got the shovels, gloves, and snacks covered!

This is a great way to earn community service hours or just de-stress before midterms. If you plan to attend, please register on the Student Life portal by Thursday so we can order enough pizza.

See you in the dirt!
— The Campus Green Team
